content.full_text:
July 26, 1976 
Mr. Ed Boyd 
united Organization of Taxpayers 
345 Bellefontaine Street 
Pasadena, California 
91~05 
Dear Mr. Boyd: 
This letter will serve to amend the advice given to you in our 
telephone conversation of Friday, July 23, 1976. 
You stated that your organization engaged in the following 
activities during the primary election: It purchased an ad in 
the newspaper, costing more than $500, urging the voters to 
support certain measures ~appearing on the primary ballot; members 
of the organization wrota ballot arguments on behalf of measures 
on the primary ballot; and, it is currently the proponent of a 
local ballot measure. 
Since your organization made a "non-
monetary" contribution in excess of $500, it must file campaign 
statements as an expenditure committee in connection with the 
primary election. 
The contribution was made on June 4, 1976. 
The report, therefore, is due on August 12, 1976 covering the 
period from January 7, 1975 through August 5, 1976 and must 
include all contributions or expenditures made for the purpose 
of influencing the action of the voters during that period. 
If your organization, as .a proponent, plans to make no further 
expenditure., receive no contributions and all liabilities 
have been paid, you may file a combined proponent and post-
election report on August 12, including all activity during the 
period stated above. 
If, however, you anticipate activity 
related to the petition drive after August 5, separate reports 
must be filed. 
The report due in connection with the election 
must contain all activity including expenditures and receipts 
in connection with the petition circulation and expenditures 
made in an effort to influence the action of the voters. 


Mr. Ed Boyd 
-2-
July 26, 1976 
Your organization will be a committee for the calendar year and 
any expenditures or contributions made this year after the filing 
of these two reports (or a combined report) will require the 
filing of additional campaign statements. 
For your convenience, 
I am enclosing a filing schedule for both ballot measure committees 
and candidate's committees. 
If your organization has no activity after satisfying your current 
filing requirements as a proponent and as an expenditure committee 
for the primary election, no additional campaign statements are 
required to be filed. 
The writing of ballot arguments in no way influences whether or 
not the United Organization of Taxpayers must file campaign state-
ments as a committee under the Political Reform Act. 
Please let me know if I may be of additional assistance. 
RSK:mak 
Enclosure 
bee: 
Jeanne 
Very truly yours, 
REGINA SICILIMJO-KUTCHINS 
Technical Assistance Program 
Coordinator

sections.question_synthetic: Must the United Organization of Taxpayers file campaign statements as an expenditure committee after making non-monetary contributions exceeding $500 to influence primary election voters, and what are the filing deadlines and reporting requirements?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Yes, the organization must file campaign statements as an expenditure committee due to non-monetary contributions exceeding $500. A report covering January 7, 1975 through August 5, 1976 is due August 12, 1976, and must include all contributions and expenditures made to influence voter action during that period.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Amendment letter to prior telephone advice. No formal QUESTION/CONCLUSION sections marked with headers, but content clearly structured as Q&A. Extracted question and conclusion from body text. Letter provides specific filing deadlines (August 12, 1976) and reporting period (January 7, 1975 - August 5, 1976).

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Must the United Organization of Taxpayers file campaign statements as an expenditure committee after making non-monetary contributions exceeding $500 to influence primary election voters, and what are the filing deadlines and reporting requirements?

CONCLUSION: Yes, the organization must file campaign statements as an expenditure committee due to non-monetary contributions exceeding $500. A report covering January 7, 1975 through August 5, 1976 is due August 12, 1976, and must include all contributions and expenditures made to influence voter action during that period.

embedding.summary: FPPC amends prior telephone advice to United Organization of Taxpayers regarding their campaign reporting obligations after making newspaper advertisements and ballot arguments exceeding $500 during the primary election. The organization must file as an expenditure committee with specific deadlines and reporting requirements for activities during the specified period.
